compatibleScales.musicalContext: D Dorian is the natural ii mode in C major. The major 6th (B) creates brighter, jazzier minor sound essential for ii chords.
compatibleScales.commonUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Essential for jazz, pop, R&B, and neo-soul. Perfect for Dm7-G7-Cmaj7 progressions.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Emphasize the B (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I progressions (Dm7-G7-Cmaj7). Smooth voice leading.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: All C major notes work over Em since it's diatonic. Maintains key center. Simple and effective.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over iii. Simple diatonic approach. Works universally.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Think C major, emphasize E as root. The F creates natural Phrygian flavor. Maintains tonal clarity.

compatibleScales.relationship: vi scale - relative minor, shares all notes with B major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: G# minor is the relative minor of B major; they share all seven notes. Provides the minor color within the B major key system
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Natural minor with Bb instead of B. Creates traditional, somber minor sound. More melancholic than Dorian.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. Perfect for melancholic sections. More traditional than Dorian. Common in emotional rock and classical contexts.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: The Bb (b13) creates darker color. Use for traditional sad minor sound. Common in rock ballads and classical.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Safe minor pentatonic removes harmonic complexity. Universal minor sound without modal color.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Perfect for simple, bluesy ii minor. Works in rock, blues, and pop. Safe choice for improvisation without modal sophistication.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Very safe and simple. Perfect for straightforward minor melodies. Great for beginners and bluesy approaches.

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
compatibleScales.musicalContext: E Phrygian is the iii mode of C major. The b2 (F) creates Spanish/exotic character. Less common but effective for drama.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor. Creates drama in C major. Common in progressive rock and flamenco.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Emphasize the b2 (F) for Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality. Perfect for tension and exotic flavor.

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative minor pentatonic - vi relationship to F major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: D minor pentatonic shares many notes with F major pentatonic and works beautifully over F major progressions, adding minor color
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use to add minor flavor over F major progressions. Perfect for creating tension/release in major key songs
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Use to add minor flavor over F major progressions. Perfect for creating tension/release in major key songs
